source.raw_text: Eine Radfahrerin befuhr den rechten Fahrstreifen der Nebenfahrbahn Unter den Eichen und fuhr bei Rot in den Kreuzungsbereich Unter den Eichen / Drakestraße / Habelschwerdter Allee ein. Dabei kam es zur Kollision mit einem Pkw, der den linken Fahrstreifen der Drakestraße in Richtung Unter den Eichen befuhr.

location.primary_road: Unter den Eichen Nebenfahrbahn to Drakestraße approach
location.secondary_road: Drakestraße to Unter den Eichen approach
location.osm_query: Unter den Eichen Drakestraße Habelschwerdter Allee, Berlin, Germany
osm_query_candidates: Unter den Eichen Drakestraße, Berlin, Germany
Drakestraße Unter den Eichen, Berlin, Germany
Habelschwerdter Allee Unter den Eichen, Berlin, Germany
direction_references: from Unter den Eichen toward Drakestraße
from Drakestraße toward Unter den Eichen

road_context.number_of_lanes_mentioned: cyclist right lane, car left lane

primary_heading_rad.reason: The screenshot shows the cyclist on the Unter den Eichen Nebenfahrbahn travelling along the side roadway direction. Nearby OSM segments contain both directions of the divided road, so the side-road heading is selected manually from map review.

secondary_road_lanes.reason: Manual review of the OSM/imagery screenshot confirms that the report-relevant Drakestraße approach is a broad four-lane approach. The Pkw is placed on the leftmost of these lanes.

secondary_forward_lanes.reason: The report places the Pkw on the left Fahrstreifen of Drakestraße in the direction of Unter den Eichen, so the generated approach needs two lanes in the Pkw travel direction.

secondary_opposite_lanes.reason: The map screenshot shows the remaining Drakestraße lanes as the opposite-direction side of the same street; they are kept as visual road-topology context.

conflict.collision_description: Cyclist enters the signalized intersection on red from the right lane of the Unter den Eichen side roadway and collides with a passenger car travelling from Drakestraße toward Unter den Eichen.

missing_parameters.reason: The supplied report excerpt gives the date as unknown.
Cyclist speed was not provided in the report.
OSM maxspeed=50.0 km/h was found near the location; the simulation uses 65% of the limit as an intersection approach speed.

missing_parameters.value_used: cyclist and car start upstream of the conflict point on their report-relevant approach lanes
missing_parameters.reason: Exact starting positions are not in the report; they are chosen so both actors reach the red-point conflict area at t=4s. Lane ids may be refined by OSM/manual map review before generation.

missing_parameters.value_used: skewed crossing abstraction with Unter den Eichen side roadway, Drakestraße 2+2 lane topology, and visual context roads for the divided B1 carriageway
missing_parameters.reason: The exact junction geometry is more complex than a simple two-road crossing; OSM and the map screenshot are used to preserve the report-relevant approach directions and lane topology while keeping the prototype runnable in esmini.

OSM lane tags were retained as evidence, but at least one approach lane count was manually overridden because the OSM intersection segment did not cleanly represent the report-relevant directional lane group.
Cyclist OpenSCENARIO lane id follows the chosen lateral-position policy so the initial teleport matches the generated trajectory.
The report explicitly places the cyclist on a motor-vehicle lane.

osm_context.query: Unter den Eichen Drakestraße Habelschwerdter Allee, Berlin, Germany
queries_tried: Unter den Eichen Drakestraße Habelschwerdter Allee, Berlin, Germany
notes: OSM turn:lanes tags were kept as evidence only; the report does not explicitly describe a left-turn maneuver for car_1.

geocoded.display_name: Unter den Eichen/Drakestraße, Habelschwerdter Allee, Dahlem, Steglitz-Zehlendorf, Berlin, 14195, Deutschland

lane_count_evidence.policy: For police-report approaches, use OSM lanes only from way segments whose name matches the 'from' road and whose geometry moves toward the 'toward' road. Nearby road segments are kept as evidence but are not averaged.

secondary_road_lanes.reason: Manual review of the OSM/imagery screenshot confirms that the report-relevant Drakestraße approach is a broad four-lane approach. The Pkw is placed on the leftmost of these lanes.

secondary_forward_lanes.reason: The report places the Pkw on the left Fahrstreifen of Drakestraße in the direction of Unter den Eichen, so the generated approach needs two lanes in the Pkw travel direction.

secondary_opposite_lanes.reason: The map screenshot shows the remaining Drakestraße lanes as the opposite-direction side of the same street; they are kept as visual road-topology context.

primary_heading_rad.reason: The screenshot shows the cyclist on the Unter den Eichen Nebenfahrbahn travelling along the side roadway direction. Nearby OSM segments contain both directions of the divided road, so the side-road heading is selected manually from map review.

cyclist_lateral_position.policy: default rightmost unless report or OSM indicate left/middle.
